page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and the Nitro hypervisor.

In terms of performance, the c6gn.2xlarge demonstrates average capabilities across several multi-core and memory benchmarks. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 12130.94 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5435.0. Memory bandwidth is notable, reaching 472.59 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 119.78 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 946.0 and a passmark CPU score of 5203.69. Cryptographic and compression tasks also show lower efficiency, with a passmark encryption score of 1082.7 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 20.35 GB/sec.

The instance is optimized for network-heavy workloads, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. It performs reliably in web-serving and database operations, delivering 9.68 GB/sec in static web serving throughput and handling 9.2 million Redis SET operations per second. Conversely, it is poorly suited for large language model inference, managing only 38.56 tokens per second for text generation with a small 135M model. This hardware profile provides a cost-efficient option for ARM-native applications, software compilation, and network-bound services that benefit from dedicated physical cores without requiring high memory-to-CPU ratios.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12130.94 and a static web serving throughput of 9.68 GB/sec, though single-core and cryptographic performance are relatively weak. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for ARM-native workloads, software compilation, and network-bound services like Redis caching, where it handles 9.2 million SET operations per second, but it is not recommended for intensive machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, ideal for network-bound and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and high network bandwidth for web serving and network-bound workloads.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores
16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps optimized for network-bound tasks
Average multi-core performance suitable for software compilation and web serving
Weak single-core and cryptographic performance relative to x86 alternatives
Best suited for ARM-native applications, caching, and lightweight database operations
